Class Player

java.lang.Object
jogo.models.Player
public class Player extends Object
Represents a player within the game session. Manages the player's unique identity, score tracking, Action Points (AP), and an inventory of various material resources.
Author:
Fabio Cruz, Tiago Silva

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors

    Description

    Constructs a new Player with default starting resources, score, and Action Points.

  • Constructor Details

    • Player

      public Player(String name)
      Constructs a new Player with default starting resources, score, and Action Points.
      Parameters:
      name - The unique name of the player.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the provided name is empty or null.

    • Player

      @Deprecated public Player(String name, int score, int wood, int stone, int food, int actionPoints)
      Deprecated.
      Constructs a Player instance with explicit values for all attributes. Primarily utilized by the data persistence layer when restoring a saved game state.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the player.
      score - The player's accumulated score.
      wood - The initial amount of wood resource.
      stone - The initial amount of stone resource.
      food - The initial amount of food resource.
      actionPoints - The current available Action Points.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the provided name is empty or null.

    • Player

      public Player(String name, int score, int wood, int stone, int food, int currentAp, int maxBaseAp)
      Advanced reconstruction constructor for the data persistence layer. Separates volatile current turn AP from the permanent maximum baseline AP capacity.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the player.
      score - The player's accumulated score.
      wood - The current amount of wood resource.
      stone - The current amount of stone resource.
      food - The current amount of food resource.
      currentAp - The current active Action Points remaining in the turn.
      maxBaseAp - The maximum baseline Action Points capacity for turn initialization.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the provided name is empty or null.

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()

    • getActionPoints

      public int getActionPoints()

    • getInventory

      public Map<ResourceType, Integer> getInventory()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the player's current resource inventory.
      Returns:
      A read-only Map containing the resource types and their quantities.

    • getScore

      public int getScore()

    • getBaseActionPoints

      public int getBaseActionPoints()

    • resetAP

      public void resetAP()
      Resets the player's current Action Points in the inventory back to their baseline value. Typically triggered at the start of a new game turn.

    • addBaseActionPoints

      public void addBaseActionPoints(int value)
      Permanently increases the player's maximum baseline Action Points and updates the current available pool.
      Parameters:
      value - The amount of baseline points to add.

    • removeBaseActionPoints

      public void removeBaseActionPoints(int value)
      Permanently decreases the player's maximum baseline Action Points and updates the current available pool.
      Parameters:
      value - The amount of baseline points to decrease.

    • addScore

      public void addScore(int value)
      Increments the player's total victory score.
      Parameters:
      value - The points to be added to the score.

    • getResourceQuantity

      public int getResourceQuantity(ResourceType resource)
      Retrieves the current quantity of a specific resource stored in the inventory.
      Parameters:
      resource - The ResourceType to query.
      Returns:
      The integer amount of the resource, or 0 if it is not present.

    • addResource

      public void addResource(ResourceType resource, int quantity)
      Adds a specified quantity of a resource to the player's inventory.
      Parameters:
      resource - The ResourceType to accumulate.
      quantity - The positive amount to add.

    • removeResource

      public boolean removeResource(ResourceType resource, int quantity)
      Attempts to deduct a specified quantity of a resource from the inventory. Fails if the player possesses insufficient quantities.
      Parameters:
      resource - The ResourceType to consume.
      quantity - The amount required for the operation.
      Returns:
      true if the consumption succeeded; false if resources were insufficient.

    • clearInventory

      public void clearInventory()
      Sets the quantity of all valid in-game resources to zero.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

      public static int getInitialActionPoints()

    • getInitialItems

      public static int getInitialItems()

    • getInitialScore

      public static int getInitialScore()
